task: Find the most frequent word that rhymes with "thought" and has three syllables.
thoughts: I need to find the rhyming dictionary on Merriam-Webster. Let me navigate to their thesaurus section which often includes rhyming features.
I see the thesaurus page but I need the rhyming dictionary. Let me look for it on Merriam-Webster.
The rhyming URL didn't work. Let me look for the Word Finder feature which might include rhyming capabilities.
Let me check the dropdown options - the "Starting with" dropdown might have a rhyming option.
No rhyming option in Word Finder. Let me dismiss this and check the dictionary entry for "thought" which may link to rhyming words.
No rhyming option in Word Finder. Let me dismiss this and check the dictionary entry for "thought" which may link to rhyming words.
I can see a "Rhymes" link in the left sidebar. Let me click on it.
I can see rhymes for "thought" but only one-syllable words so far. Let me click "See All Rhymes for thought" to see the complete list including three-syllable words.
Excellent! I found the RhymeZone rhyming dictionary page. It shows 1093 rhymes grouped by syllables and sorted by frequency. I need to scroll down to find the 3-syllable words section.
I still see 1-syllable words. Let me scroll down more to reach the 3-syllable section.
I see 2-syllable words now. Let me keep scrolling to find the 3-syllable section.
Still in 2-syllable words. Let me scroll down much more to reach the 3-syllable section.
Still in 2-syllable words. Let me continue scrolling down.
I can see what looks like 3-syllable words. Let me scroll up a bit to find the "3 syllables" header and see the most frequent words first.

I found the "3 syllables" section. The words are sorted by frequency, with the darkest green being most frequent. Let me get a closer look at the top of this section and the frequency colors to identify the most frequent one.
I can see the 3-syllable section clearly now. The words are sorted by frequency with color coding. Let me click on "Advanced View" on the left sidebar to get more detailed frequency information.

Excellent! The Advanced View shows detailed data with columns for Word, Rhyme rating, Syllables, and Popularity. Let me click on the "Syllables" column to sort by number of syllables to find the 3-syllable words.
I see 1-syllable words (shown as "/"). Let me click the Syllables header again to sort in descending order so 3-syllable words appear first.

Now I can see 3-syllable words (shown as "xx/" syllables). I see "overwrought" with the highest popularity. Let me now sort by Popularity to find the most frequent 3-syllable word. Let me click the Popularity column header.

The list is now sorted by popularity (descending). I can see 1-syllable words at the top. I need to scroll down to find the first 3-syllable word ("xx/" in Syllables column) - that will be the most frequent one.
Still seeing 1 and 2-syllable words. Let me continue scrolling to find the first 3-syllable word (xx/).
The results are paginated. The 3-syllable words are on later pages since they're less popular. Let me go to page 2.
Let me scroll to the top of page 2 to see all the entries.
Let me try using the Syllable Stress filter to narrow to 3-syllable words. Let me click on the Syllable Stress dropdown.

I see the syllable stress patterns. For 3-syllable words, I need to check patterns like "/xx" (dactylic), "x/x" (amphibrach), and "xx/" (anapaest). Let me select "/xx" (dactylic) first as that's the most common pattern for 3-syllable words ending in the rhyme sound.
Let me try clicking more precisely on the Syllable Stress dropdown.
Good, the dropdown is open. Let me select "/xx (dactylic)" - the 3-syllable pattern for words like "astronaut".
0 results for /xx pattern. Let me try "xx/" (anapaest) which is the pattern I saw earlier for 3-syllable words like "overwrought".
0 results for /xx pattern. Let me try "xx/" (anapaest) which is the pattern I saw earlier for 3-syllable words like "overwrought".

I see 9 results for xx/ (anapaest) 3-syllable words. "anymore" has the highest popularity but only an 86 rhyme rating. "overwrought" has a 100 rhyme rating. Let me scroll down to see all 9 results, and then I'll check the other 3-syllable pattern too.

I see all 9 results for xx/ pattern. "overwrought" has a perfect rhyme rating of 100. But I need to check the other 3-syllable patterns too. Let me also check "Rare words" to include more words, and try the x/x pattern. Let me first enable "Rare words".
Let me reset the filter to "All Results" and sort by syllables to see all 3-syllable words with their popularity.
Let me reset the filter to "All Results" and sort by syllables to see all 3-syllable words with their popularity.
Let me go back to the main Rhymes view to see the full 3-syllable list. Let me click "Rhymes 1093" in the sidebar.
Good, I'm back on the main Rhymes page with the frequency color legend. Let me scroll down to the 3-syllable section to compare the color shading of words there.

Now I can clearly see the 3-syllable section with the color-coded frequency. Looking at the boxes carefully, I can see different shades of green. Let me click on one of the words to see its frequency details. Let me try clicking on "astronaut" first.
